_readme: FAMTEC demonstration collection records for the archai_famtec Qdrant collection. Authored independently by FAMTEC from publicly documented product history — no source-institution record reproduced. To make live: embed each record's embedding_text with nomic-embed-text and upsert to the archai_famtec collection, then regenerate AUXIO pages.

objects.title: Sound Burger — Audio-Technica AT727 Portable Turntable
objects.medium: ABS polymer clamshell housing, belt-drive motor, moving-magnet stereo cartridge, 3.5 mm headphone output, battery or DC power
objects.description: The Audio-Technica AT727 'Sound Burger' is a portable belt-drive record player released in 1983, at the height of the Walkman era's race to make music personal and portable. Its clamshell body clamps a record between hub and tonearm so the disc protrudes from both sides of the machine like a patty in a bun — the shape that earned its nickname. Unlike most novelty portables it is a genuine hi-fi component in miniature: belt drive, a moving-magnet cartridge, two speeds (33⅓ and 45 rpm), battery or mains power, and a headphone output that let listeners take full-size vinyl anywhere. Production of the original run ended in the late 1980s as cassette and CD took the portable market, and surviving units became collectors' items. In 2022 Audio-Technica reissued the design for its 60th anniversary — the limited AT-SB2022, followed by the AT-SB727 — adding Bluetooth and USB-C charging while keeping the 1983 silhouette almost unchanged: a rare case of a forty-year-old industrial design returning to production on the strength of its original idea.
objects.wall_description: A record player you could carry. The 1983 Sound Burger clamps a full-size LP between its jaws and plays it on batteries, anywhere — vinyl's answer to the Walkman.
objects.fun_fact: The record sticks out of both sides of the player while it spins — the 'burger' look is the whole engineering idea, not a styling joke.
objects.did_you_know: Audio-Technica brought the Sound Burger back for its 60th anniversary in 2022; the limited reissue sold out so quickly it returned as a permanent product, the AT-SB727, in 2023.
objects.provenance: Demonstration record authored by FAMTEC in 2026 from publicly documented product history, for ARCHAI research demonstration. This record describes the AT727 model; no individual unit's ownership history is recorded.
objects.tombstone: Audio-Technica AT727 'Sound Burger', portable belt-drive turntable, Japan, 1983.
rights_notes: Record authored independently by FAMTEC for research demonstration; no source-institution record reproduced.
Image is an original FAMTEC illustration (CC BY 4.0), created for this record — not an Audio-Technica product photograph.
verified_facts: Released by Audio-Technica in 1983 as the AT727 (with AT770 variant)
Portable belt-drive record player with clamshell clamping design
Plays 33⅓ and 45 rpm records; moving-magnet cartridge; headphone output; battery or DC power
Nicknamed 'Sound Burger' for the way the disc protrudes from the housing
Reissued for Audio-Technica's 60th anniversary in 2022 (AT-SB2022), then as the AT-SB727 with Bluetooth and USB-C
unknown_fields: This record describes the model, not an individual unit: no specific unit's owners, locations, service history, or records played are documented
curator_approved: My records say I'm a 1983 model, but honestly I don't know where I've been or what I've played.
prohibited_statements: Claims about named individuals or specific owners
Invented memories of particular events, parties, or places
Claims about any specific unit's history
